source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Hercules 300 Structural Damage / Chemical Release

actions_taken: Derrickman turned off the centrifugal pump to stop the flow of fluid overboard
Derrickman notified the Arena Rig Site Supervisor
event.summary: On 13-Nov-2014, the Hercules 300 was completing well A5 when approximately 66 barrels of 16.6 ppg Zinc Bromide completion fluid was discharged overboard. The incident occurred because the valve on the cement unit from the fluid delivery line to the mud pits was left open, allowing Zinc Bromide to enter the displacement tank of the cement unit. The drain valve on the displacement tank was also in the open position, allowing the fluid to discharge overboard. The Derrickman failed to confirm that valves isolating the cement unit were closed prior to beginning the fluid transfer to the filtration unit.
key_phrases: failure to follow JSA risk reduction measures

threats.name: Valve left in open position on cement unit
Drain valve on displacement tank left in open position
Failure to confirm valve isolation prior to fluid transfer

consequences.name: Overboard discharge of Zinc Bromide completion fluid

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) preparation and review
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify and communicate risk reduction measures

supporting_text: The Derrickman prepared a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) which was reviewed and signed by the OIM and the Driller on tour
The failures of the Derrickman to follow the risk reduction measures outlined on the JSA

controls.name: Valve isolation verification prior to fluid transfer
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure isolation of unintended fluid pathways

supporting_text: confirm the valves isolating the cement unit were closed prior to beginning the Transfer of fluid to the filtration unit
The valve on cement unit from the fluid delivery line to the mud pits was open, allowing the Zinc Bromide to go into the displacement tank of the cement unit

controls.name: Communication between Derrickman and Pro-T filtration employee
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to coordinate fluid transfer operations

supporting_text: Prior to commencing the transfer he called the Pro-T filtration employee and told him he was ready to transfer fluid. The Pro-T employee gave the okay to start the pump and begin the transfer
After a few minutes elapsed without observing returns in the mud room, the Derrickman went to the filtration unit to investigate why

controls.name: Monitoring of fluid return in mud pit room
controls.barrier_role: Detection control to identify abnormal fluid flow conditions

supporting_text: The Derrickman started the centrifugal pump and waited in the mud pit room monitoring the return line to confirm fluid was circulating to the filtration unit and being returned to the correct mud pit
After a few minutes elapsed without observing returns in the mud room, the Derrickman went to the filtration unit to investigate why

controls.name: Investigation and corrective action by Derrickman
controls.barrier_role: Recovery control to stop fluid discharge and notify supervision

supporting_text: The Derrickman went to the Halliburton cement unit to check the valves on the line to the cement unit's displacement tanks
The Derrickman discovered the valve on cement unit from the fluid delivery line to the mud pits was open
The Derrickman returned to the pit room and turned off the centrifugal pump, stopping the flow of fluid overboard and notified the Arena Rig Site Supervisor

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
